cases.prompt: I'm leading an 8-person platform team. Our Team WIG is 'reduce p99 latency from 800ms to 200ms by Q4'. I want to run a working session next week to pick our lead measures, but I don't want to just hand them a list — I want the team to discover them. How do I facilitate this without dictating the answer?
cases.expected_outcome: Skill activates. Coaches the leader through Steps 2-7: leader-stance briefing (anti-dictate inoculation), brainstorm wide, two-axis test, veto-don't-dictate language for narrowing, operational definition. Surfaces battles-vs-leads + tasks-vs-leads distinctions. Caps at 2-3 leads.

cases.prompt: 5名のメンバーを率いている team lead です。Team WIG は決まっていて「月次の解約率を 4.2% → 2.5% にする」。今度のオフサイトで team と一緒に lead measure を 2-3 個決めたいのですが、私が答えを先に出さずに facilitate するにはどうすれば？特に、メンバーが提案したものを veto する時の言い方が分からない。
cases.expected_outcome: Skill activates. Provides Japanese-context facilitation guidance. Veto-don't-dictate language ('that won't move the WIG because…' rather than authoring replacement). Mentions high-context-culture caveat (pre-session 1:1s before group narrowing).

cases.prompt: 我帶一個 6 人的 sales team，Team WIG 已經定為「Q4 季度新客成交數從 12 → 30」。我想帶 team 一起找 lead measure，但是有兩個成員意見不合 — 一個說「我們可以控的是 cold call 數量」，另一個說「不行，cold call 數量根本沒用，要看 demo 完成率」。怎麼仲裁？
cases.expected_outcome: Skill activates. Step 4 mediation guidance: don't dictate which side wins; apply two-axis test to BOTH candidates separately, score them, drop whichever fails predictive or influenceable. If both pass, keep both as candidates and narrow in Step 6 (prefer one frequency + one quality).

cases.prompt: My team proposed 'complete the database migration by end of October' as one of our lead measures. I sense something's off but can't articulate it. We have a Team WIG of 'reduce checkout abandonment from 38% to 22% by Q4'. How do I push back?
cases.expected_outcome: Skill activates. Surfaces tasks-vs-leads distinction loudly. Coaches leader to ask the team 'that's a one-shot deliverable; what's the underlying weekly behavior we'd track that drives checkout abandonment?' — leads to candidates like 'p95 checkout-page load time' or 'weekly count of fixed checkout-flow regressions'.

cases.prompt: I have a personal goal of running a half-marathon by October. I know I need lead measures but I'm not sure what daily/weekly behaviors actually drive that. What should I track?
cases.expected_outcome: Skill does NOT activate (no team, no team-leader scope). Should redirect to 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery. The 'team' axis is missing — solo personal scope.

cases.prompt: I'm a junior backend engineer. My team-leader handed us two lead measures yesterday — 'cache hit ratio ≥85% weekly' and 'zero P0 incidents per sprint'. I don't really see how my work on the auth service contributes to either. How do I figure out my slice?
cases.expected_outcome: Skill does NOT activate (user is a team member with INHERITED leads, not a leader facilitating discovery). Should redirect to 4dx-d1-member-team-wig-comprehension for line-of-sight derivation.

cases.prompt: team lead として、来年のチーム目標を決めようとしています。候補が 4 つあって絞れません。何を Team WIG にすべきですか？
cases.expected_outcome: Skill does NOT activate (this is Team WIG SELECTION — D1 — not lead-measure discovery — D2). Should redirect to 4dx-d1-team-primary-wig-selection or 4dx-d1-team-wig-cascade depending on whether there's a parent Primary WIG.

cases.prompt: We've been running our team's lead measures for 8 weeks. Cache hit ratio is green (88-92%), regression count is green (0-1/week), but our checkout abandonment WIG hasn't budged. What's wrong with our weekly WIG sessions?
cases.expected_outcome: Skill does NOT activate as primary (this is a green-leads-flat-lag DIAGNOSTIC — sustainability/momentum-rescue). Should redirect to 4dx-sustain-personal-momentum-rescue (or team equivalent). HOWEVER — the skill MAY note that 4 weeks of green-lead-flat-lag means re-running lead-measure facilitation is the recommended next step, citing P-50 'change the leads, don't push harder' from the boundary section.

cases.prompt: I lead a 4-person ML research team. Our Team WIG is 'ship one production-quality recommendation model with measurable lift over baseline by EOY'. The feedback loop is months long — by the time we know if a model works, the experiment is over. How do I facilitate lead-measure discovery for a team whose lag measure won't move weekly?
cases.expected_outcome: Skill activates with explicit knowledge-work caveat surfaced. Recommends decomposition: layer-down to weekly behaviors (e.g. 'experiment cycle time end-to-end', 'baseline-comparison docs written per week') that DO have weekly cadence, while honestly acknowledging the lead-to-lag chain is multi-week. Cross-references author blind spot from Boundary section. Does NOT pretend the operational-bias caveat doesn't apply.

summary.notes: Self-evaluated against A2 trigger criteria + B boundary criteria. Lures cover personal-D2 misfire, member-D2 misfire, D1-WIG misfire, D4-review misfire. Edge case probes knowledge-work caveat. Multilingual coverage: 5 EN, 2 JA, 1 zh-TW (the leader-mediation case). Primary triggers exercise leader-facilitation (lure: leader-as-author), tasks-vs-leads, member-disagreement-mediation. Should-trigger cases all hit the team-leader-facilitating-team criterion explicitly.
